rules.name: no-mixed-commits
rules.evidence: Never mix structural and behavioral changes in the same commit

rules.name: commit-preconditions
rules.evidence: Only commit when: 1. ALL tests are passing 2. ALL compiler/linter warnings have been resolved 3. The change represents a single logical unit of work 4. Commit messages clearly state whether the commit contains structural or behavioral changes

rules.name: prefer-small-commits
rules.evidence: Use small, frequent commits rather than large, infrequent ones

uncompilable: Follow the TDD cycle Red -> Green -> Refactor -- general methodology, no specific tool call
Write the simplest failing test first -- general test-writing style, not action-bindable
Implement the minimum code needed to make tests pass -- general coding style guidance
Refactor only after tests are passing -- ordering guidance with no matchable tool/pattern
Separate all changes into structural vs behavioral changes -- general categorization, not itself an action
Always make structural changes first when both are needed -- ordering guidance, no matchable trigger
Validate structural changes do not alter behavior by running tests before and after -- generic test-running advice, no bindable pattern
Code quality standards (eliminate duplication, express intent, minimize state, etc.) -- general coding style, not action-bindable
Refactoring guidelines (one change at a time, run tests after each step, use named patterns) -- general process guidance, no specific tool/pattern
When fixing a defect, write an API-level failing test then the smallest replicating test -- workflow guidance, not tied to a specific tool/pattern
Always follow the instructions in plan.md; when I say 'go', find next unmarked test and implement it -- session workflow triggered by conversational cue, not a Bash/Edit/Write action
Always run all the tests (except long-running tests) each time -- advisory test-running cadence, no clean tool/pattern binding without over-triggering
Example workflow narrative (write test, implement, run tests, tidy, commit separately) -- restates already-captured guidance, not a distinct new bindable action
